meta.name: BRS Brief Resilience Scale
meta.description: The BRS is a 6-item scale assessing the ability to bounce back or recover from stress. Resilience is the capacity to recover from difficult experiences. The scale comprises 3 positively worded and 3 negatively worded items measuring core resilience in coping with adversity.
meta.author: Smith et al. (2008)
meta.reference: Smith BW, Dalen J, Wiggins K, Steger MF, et al. The brief resilience scale: assessing the ability to bounce back. Int J Behav Med. 2008;15(3):194-200.
meta.instruction: Please indicate the extent to which you agree with each of the following statements.

questions.text: I tend to bounce back quickly after hard times
I have a hard time making it through stressful events
It does not take me long to recover from a stressful event
It is hard for me to snap back when something bad happens
I usually come through difficult times with little trouble
I tend to take a long time to get over set-backs in my life

ranges.label: Low Resilience
ranges.description: Your resilience level is low, indicating significant difficulty recovering from stress and adversity. You may feel overwhelmed by setbacks and take a long time to regain emotional balance after challenges.
suggestions: Learn and regularly practice stress management techniques such as deep breathing and mindfulness
Actively build a social support network by connecting with friends, family, or community groups
Strengthen resilience gradually through consistent exercise, adequate sleep, and balanced nutrition
If you consistently struggle to cope with setbacks, seeking professional counseling is recommended
Start small — focus on recovering from minor daily stressors before tackling larger life challenges

ranges.label: Below Average Resilience
ranges.description: Your resilience is below average. While you can cope with some stressors, more demanding situations may leave you feeling depleted or stuck. Building stronger recovery skills can improve your well-being.
suggestions: Develop a repertoire of positive coping strategies including journaling, physical activity, and social engagement
Maintain consistent sleep and exercise routines, as these form the biological foundation of resilience
Practice acceptance and mindfulness to reduce emotional overreaction to difficult situations
Identify specific activities and practices that help you recharge and schedule them regularly
Consider reading about or attending workshops on resilience-building and adaptive coping skills

ranges.label: Normal Resilience
ranges.description: You have a normal level of resilience and can generally recover well from most stressors and adversity. You bounce back from setbacks within a reasonable timeframe and maintain functional coping under pressure.
suggestions: Continue maintaining and strengthening your existing resilience resources and coping strategies
When facing new types of challenges, flexibly adapt your proven approaches to fit the situation
Nurture your positive social relationships and healthy lifestyle habits that sustain your resilience
Consider setting slightly more ambitious recovery goals to further build your resilience capacity

ranges.label: High Resilience
ranges.description: You demonstrate high resilience and can recover quickly and effectively from stress and adversity. Your strong bounce-back ability is a powerful psychological asset that protects your overall well-being.
suggestions: Continue maintaining the habits and mindset that underpin your strong resilience
Your experience and perspective can help and encourage others who struggle with setbacks
While supporting others, remember to also monitor and care for your own mental health needs
